title: Lakshmi Narayan Temple, Agartala
tagline: Lakshmi Narayan Temple, Agartala — spiritual town of Tripura

overview.short: Lakshmi Narayan Temple is a Hindu temple, dedicated to the Hindu divine couple Lakshmi Narayana.
overview.description: Lakshmi Narayan Temple is a Hindu temple, dedicated to the Hindu divine couple Lakshmi Narayana. It is located in the Ujjayanta Palace ground, in the city of Agartala, Tripura state, India. It was founded by the King of Tripura, Birendra Kishore Manikya, during his reign from 1909 to 1923.

topPlaces.name: Tripura (princely state)
topPlaces.description: Tripura State, also known as Hill Tipperah, was a princely state in India during the period of the British Raj and for some two years after the departure of the British. Its rulers belonged to the Manikya dynasty and until August 1947 the state was in a subsidiary alliance, from which it was released by the Indian Independence Act 1947.

topPlaces.name: Ujjayanta Palace
topPlaces.description: Ujjayanta Palace is the state museum of the Indian state of Tripura and former royal palace of the princely state of Tripura. It was built by Maharaja Radha Kishore Manikya in 1901.

topPlaces.name: Agartala
topPlaces.description: Agartala (, Bengali: [ˈagɔɾˌtɔla] , Kok Borok: [aguli]) is the capital and the largest city of the Indian state of Tripura, situated on the banks of Haora/Saidra River, about 2 kilometres (1.2 mi) east of the border with Bangladesh and about 2,499 km (1,552 mi) from the national capital, New Delhi. According to 2022 AMC data, Agartala is the second most populous city after Guwahati in Northeast India.

topPlaces.name: Tripura
topPlaces.description: Tripura () is a state in northeastern India. The third-smallest state in the country, it covers 10,491 km2 (4,051 sq mi); and the seventh-least populous state with a population of 3.67 million.

topPlaces.name: 2008 Agartala bombings
topPlaces.description: In the 2008 Agartala bombings at least 4 people were killed and a 100 injured on 1 October 2008 in a series of 5 blasts set off in Agartala, capital of the Indian state of Tripura, within 45 minutes, while two unexploded bombs were defused. This is the fourth series of blasts in India within a week, the other three being the 27 September Delhi bombing, and the Malegoan and Modasa bombings.

topPlaces.name: Umakanta Academy
topPlaces.description: Umakanta Academy is an educational institute located in Agartala, India. Established in 1890, it is the oldest school in Tripura.

topPlaces.name: Melarmath, Agartala
topPlaces.description: Melarmath is a locality in Agartala, Tripura, India.

topPlaces.name: Vivekananda Byamagar
topPlaces.description: Vivekananda Byamagar (Bengali: বিবেকানন্দ ব্যায়ামাগার) is a gymnasium, located in Agartala, Tripura, India. It is the oldest gymnasium in Agartala.

items.note: Tripura State, also known as Hill Tipperah, was a princely state in India during the period of the British Raj and for some two years after the departure of the British. Its rulers belonged to the Manikya dynasty and until August 1947 the state was in a subsidiary alliance, from which it was released by the Indian Independence Act 1947.
Ujjayanta Palace is the state museum of the Indian state of Tripura and former royal palace of the princely state of Tripura. It was built by Maharaja Radha Kishore Manikya in 1901.

Agartala (, Bengali: [ˈagɔɾˌtɔla] , Kok Borok: [aguli]) is the capital and the largest city of the Indian state of Tripura, situated on the banks of Haora/Saidra River, about 2 kilometres (1.2 mi) east of the border with Bangladesh and about 2,499 km (1,552 mi) from the national capital, New Delhi. According to 2022 AMC data, Agartala is the second most populous city after Guwahati in Northeast India.
Tripura () is a state in northeastern India. The third-smallest state in the country, it covers 10,491 km2 (4,051 sq mi); and the seventh-least populous state with a population of 3.67 million.

In the 2008 Agartala bombings at least 4 people were killed and a 100 injured on 1 October 2008 in a series of 5 blasts set off in Agartala, capital of the Indian state of Tripura, within 45 minutes, while two unexploded bombs were defused. This is the fourth series of blasts in India within a week, the other three being the 27 September Delhi bombing, and the Malegoan and Modasa bombings.
Umakanta Academy is an educational institute located in Agartala, India. Established in 1890, it is the oldest school in Tripura.
Melarmath is a locality in Agartala, Tripura, India.
Vivekananda Byamagar (Bengali: বিবেকানন্দ ব্যায়ামাগার) is a gymnasium, located in Agartala, Tripura, India. It is the oldest gymnasium in Agartala.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Lakshmi Narayan Temple, Agartala?
faq.a: The best time to visit Lakshmi Narayan Temple, Agartala is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Lakshmi Narayan Temple, Agartala?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Maharaja Bir Bikram Airport, Agartala (7 km) and the nearest railway station is Agartala Railway Station (5 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Lakshmi Narayan Temple, Agartala?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Lakshmi Narayan Temple, Agartala at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Lakshmi Narayan Temple, Agartala?
faq.a: Stays in Lakshmi Narayan Temple, Agartala start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Lakshmi Narayan Temple, Agartala?
faq.a: The top attractions are Tripura (princely state), Ujjayanta Palace, Agartala, Tripura, 2008 Agartala bombings.
